sourceMovie.title: National Security
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: National Security works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 21 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If National Security clicked for you, 21 Jump Street (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after National Security?
faq.answer: Try 21 Jump Street, Rush Hour, Rush Hour 2—each shares DNA with National Security while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is National Security?
faq.answer: National Security (2003) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is National Security worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
